title: dryopteris consanguinea
intro: Dryopteris consanguinea, also known as the Two-flanged Buckler Fern, is a species of fern in the Dryopteris genus, native to Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of D. consanguinea are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. D. consanguinea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
